name: Fruit (Overview)

book_claims.claim: Regular consumption supports disease prevention and longevity
book_claims.mechanism: Part of fibre-rich food group providing vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals processed by gut microbiome into protective metabolites

book_claims.claim: 53% reduced risk of Crohn's disease with higher consumption of vegetables and fruit
book_claims.mechanism: Pectin and other fruit fibres feed beneficial gut bacteria and produce anti-inflammatory short-chain fatty acids

book_claims.claim: Provide antioxidant vitamins (especially vitamin C) that protect against oxidative damage
book_claims.mechanism: Vitamin C neutralises free radicals, prevents LDL oxidation

supplementary_research.finding: Aune et al. (2017, BMJ): meta-analysis of 95 prospective studies found fruit consumption associated with dose-dependent reductions in cardiovascular disease, cancer, and all-cause mortality. Optimal intake appeared to be around 800g/day of combined fruit and vegetables.

China Kadoorie Biobank (Du et al., 2016): 500,000-person study found daily fresh fruit consumption associated with 40% lower cardiovascular mortality, 34% lower ischaemic heart disease, and 25% lower stroke compared to non-consumers.
GBD 2017 analysis identified low fruit intake as a leading dietary risk factor globally, responsible for approximately 3.4 million deaths annually, primarily from cardiovascular disease and cancer.

culinary_pairings.source: Traditional pairing as dessert; fat + fibre combination
Porridge and muesli; dual fibre sources
Traditional sweetener pairing for baked and fresh fruit
Warming spices complement fruit sweetness in compotes and bakes

seasonality: Eat seasonally when possible: stone fruits and berries in summer, apples and pears in autumn, citrus in winter, dried fruits year-round. Frozen fruit retains most nutrients. Fontana's protocol includes seasonal fresh fruit as dessert and dried fruit in winter.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highly bioavailable from whole fruit. Degrades with cooking and prolonged storage.
Mix of soluble (pectin) and insoluble fibre. Whole fruit food matrix critical for glycaemic control.
Anthocyanins, flavanones, ellagitannins, chlorogenic acid etc. Gut microbial metabolism produces bioactive metabolites.
Well-absorbed. Fruit is a major dietary potassium source supporting blood pressure regulation.

nutrient_highlights.amount_per_100g: Varies by colour; orange/red fruits highest
nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fat-soluble; absorption improved when eaten with healthy fats (nuts, olive oil, avocado).

synergies.mechanism: Combined fruit and vegetable intake provides diverse phytochemicals; 800g/day combined shows optimal mortality reduction
Fat from nuts improves absorption of fat-soluble vitamins and carotenoids from fruit
